comment: Copy to audit-config.json (gitignored) and fill in real targets. Each target gets a corresponding entry in 'narrative' keyed by slug.

targets.label: Example Client A — what they do

client-a.headline: One-sentence editorial headline driven by the data — appears on the PDF cover
client-a.business_one_liner: What does this company do in one neutral sentence (1 line, max 25 words).
diagnose: First paragraph: What is the overall state? Use plain language for non-SEO readers.
Second paragraph: What is the central finding? Be specific (numbers, percentages).
Third paragraph: What is the implication? What should the owner understand?
fazit: First paragraph: Overall verdict in plain language. What went well, what is broken.
Second paragraph: What it would take to fix. Realistic timeline + cost range.
Third paragraph: The one thing to do first. Make it concrete.

action_plan.why: Why does this matter — what breaks if not done
action_plan.who: Who should do this (role, not person)
action_plan.impact: What changes after this is done + timeline

client-a.intro: Run-#1-only welcome paragraph explaining the weekly cadence and that all data comes from external sources.
observations_current: Curated observation for exactly this run date — unattended runs after this date fall back to auto-generated observations.

recommendations.title: WHAT to fix (no how-to)
recommendations.text: WHY it matters and why it needs a professional — never step-by-step instructions.

client-a.mail_comment: Recipient/sender/body live in your private runner copy (weekly-runner.example.sh), never in this repo.
